taxonomy: Goeppertia contrafenestra is classified within the family Marantaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as prayer plants or prayer-brush. The genus Goeppertia itself is a member of the subfamily Marantoideae, which consists of approximately 40 genera. This classification is based on the plant's characteristic leaf structure and growth habits. As a member of the Marantaceae family, Goeppertia contrafenestra is closely related to other plants within this group, such as Calathea and Philodendron. The exact classification of Goeppertia contrafenestra may be subject to change as new research becomes available.

intro: Goeppertia contrafenestra, commonly known as the "False Window Philodendron," is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Goeppertia contrafenestra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the form of tinctures and ointments to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
